<Meta title="Brand/Assets/Icons" />

# Icons

We have designed our own set of icons.

They can only be used on websites where the City of Amsterdam is the main communicator.

We distinguish **functional icons**, which have a specific meaning and can be used without labels, from **general icons**.

Where possible, our icons have both an outlined and a filled variant.

## General icons

We name most of our icons after the objects they picture, like ‘car’, ‘house’, or ‘light bulb’.
This allows using them in various contexts or with slightly different meanings.
All these names are **nouns**.

<IconGallery
  excludeIcons={[
    ...BrandIcons,
    ...DeprecatedIcons,
    ...DirectionIcons,
    ...FormattingIcons,
    ...KeyboardKeyIcons,
    ...LearnableIcons,
    ...MediaIcons,
    ...SeverityIcons,
    ...UniversalIcons,
  ]}
/>

### Direction

Arrows and chevrons indicate direction or movement.
We use ‘forward’ and ‘backward’ to make them easier to use in languages that read from right to left.

<IconGallery icons={DirectionIcons} />

### Severity

The following icons indicate the tone of an Alert or Error Message.
These components use them without a label.

<IconGallery icons={SeverityIcons} />

### Brands

These icons can accompany links to external products or services:

<IconGallery icons={BrandIcons} />

## Functional icons

Some icons are widely recognised and can stand on their own without a label — for example, in an icon button.
Their names are usually **verbs** that describe the associated function.

**Note:** Use these icons only for the action their name represents, and don’t use another icon for such an action.

### Universal

The only icons that are recognized widely enough to be used anywhere without a label:

<IconGallery icons={UniversalIcons} />

### Learnable

In websites that users use so regularly that they can learn the meaning of icons, these can go without labels as well:

<IconGallery icons={LearnableIcons} />

### Media

The following icons can be used without labels in audio or video players:

<IconGallery icons={MediaIcons} />

### Formatting

These icons are used in text editors to indicate formatting options:

<IconGallery icons={FormattingIcons} />

### Keyboard

These icons represent common keyboard keys and can be used without labels:

<IconGallery icons={KeyboardKeyIcons} />

## Deprecated icons

These icons have been removed or renamed.
They will be fully deleted in an upcoming major release, so please update any remaining usages.

<IconGallery icons={DeprecatedIcons} />

## Related components

- [Icon](/docs/components-media-icon--docs)

## Requests

If you need an icon that is not in our set, [check this Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/SZa5JuGk9AkvAcVKJ9qu6B/Icon-Guidelines?node-id=2006-933) to contact our icon designers.

Brand/Design Tokens/Aspect Ratio

brand-design-tokens-aspect-ratio--docs · ./src/brand/design-tokens/aspect-ratio.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import { SpaceSample } from "../../_components/SpaceSample/SpaceSample";

<Meta title="Brand/Design Tokens/Aspect Ratio" />

# Aspect Ratio

Constrains media content to a supported aspect ratio.

## Design

Media content like images, videos, and maps take up sizable portions of a screen.
Displaying them in a limited set of aspect ratios keeps the visual impression of a page composed.
The default aspect ratio is 16:9.
Content with intrinsic dimensions that do not match an aspect ratio will be cropped.

## Design tokens

| CSS variable                   | Value  | Example                                           |
| :----------------------------- | :----: | :------------------------------------------------ |
| `var(--ams-aspect-ratio-9-16)` | 9 / 16 | <SpaceSample className="ams-aspect-ratio-9-16" /> |
| `var(--ams-aspect-ratio-3-4)`  | 3 / 4  | <SpaceSample className="ams-aspect-ratio-3-4" />  |
| `var(--ams-aspect-ratio-1-1)`  | 1 / 1  | <SpaceSample className="ams-aspect-ratio-1-1" />  |
| `var(--ams-aspect-ratio-4-3)`  | 4 / 3  | <SpaceSample className="ams-aspect-ratio-4-3" />  |
| `var(--ams-aspect-ratio-16-9)` | 16 / 9 | <SpaceSample className="ams-aspect-ratio-16-9" /> |
| `var(--ams-aspect-ratio-16-5)` | 16 / 5 | <SpaceSample className="ams-aspect-ratio-16-5" /> |

## Related components

- The [Image](/docs/components-media-image--docs) component has an `aspectRatio` prop that sets one of these values.
- The [Breakout](/docs/components-layout-breakout--docs) layout positions an image with a certain aspect ratio in a Spotlight.
- We provide these aspect ratios as [CSS utility classes](/docs/utilities-css-aspect-ratio--docs).

Brand/Design Tokens/Border

brand-design-tokens-border--docs · ./src/brand/design-tokens/border.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import { DesignTokensTable } from "../../_components/DesignTokensTable/DesignTokensTable";

import tokensCompact from "../../../../packages-proprietary/tokens/src/brand/ams/border.compact.tokens.json";
import tokensSpacious from "../../../../packages-proprietary/tokens/src/brand/ams/border.tokens.json";

<Meta title="Brand/Design Tokens/Border" />

# Border

Elements that have a border, outline or underline use one of these widths.

## Design

We use a limited set of border widths for consistency.

## Design tokens

### Spacious Mode

The default border is 2 pixels thick.
One thinner and two thicker options are available.

Some values have a negative equivalent for borders that are drawn inside elements, using a negative offset for a box shadow.

<DesignTokensTable tokens={tokensSpacious} />

### Compact Mode

All borders are a pixel thinner in [Compact Mode](/docs/docs-developer-guide-modes--docs), except for the small width.

<DesignTokensTable tokens={tokensCompact} />

Brand/Design Tokens/Colour

brand-design-tokens-colour--docs · ./src/brand/design-tokens/colour.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import { ColorPalette } from "../../_components/ColorPalette/ColorPalette";
import { ColorSample } from "../../_components/ColorSample/ColorSample";
import { InlineColorSample } from "../../_components/InlineColorSample/InlineColorSample";

<Meta title="Brand/Design Tokens/Colour" />

# Colour

Our colour palette is vibrant and crisp.
The main colours are red, blue, black, and white.
They are complemented by two greys and seven supplemental colours.

## Palette

The primary appearance of each colour has been called level 60.
Some colours offer a darker shade at level 80 for hover effects.
We have five neutral colours with white at level 0 and black at 100.

<ColorPalette>
  <ColorPalette.Swatch name="red">
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#ec0000" level="60" />
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#b70000" level="80" />
  </ColorPalette.Swatch>
  <ColorPalette.Swatch name="blue">
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#004699" level="60" />
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#003677" level="80" />
  </ColorPalette.Swatch>
  <ColorPalette.Swatch name="neutral">
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#ffffff" level="0" />
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#e8e8e8" level="10" />
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#d1d1d1" level="20" />
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#767676" level="60" />
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#202020" level="100" />
  </ColorPalette.Swatch>
  <ColorPalette.Swatch name="lime">
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#bed200" level="60" />
  </ColorPalette.Swatch>
  <ColorPalette.Swatch name="green">
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#00893c" level="60" />
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#10552b" level="80" />
  </ColorPalette.Swatch>
  <ColorPalette.Swatch name="orange">
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#ff9100" level="60" />
  </ColorPalette.Swatch>
  <ColorPalette.Swatch name="azure">
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#009de6" level="60" />
  </ColorPalette.Swatch>
  <ColorPalette.Swatch name="yellow">
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#ffe600" level="60" />
  </ColorPalette.Swatch>
  <ColorPalette.Swatch name="magenta">
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#e50082" level="60" />
  </ColorPalette.Swatch>
  <ColorPalette.Swatch name="purple">
    <ColorPalette.Tile color="#a00078" level="60" />
  </ColorPalette.Swatch>
</ColorPalette>

## Brand design tokens

The term ‘Brand’ represents the highest level in the three-tier token structure of the [NL Design System](https://nldesignsystem.nl/handboek/design-tokens/).

Our brand colour tokens have meaningful (semantic) names instead of generic labels like ‘blue-60’ or ‘red-100’ that other design systems may use.
This approach ensures that the correct colour is used in the appropriate context.
Always use a token for its intended purpose based on its name.
Please reach out to us if you require a different colour token.

### Text

These are the potential foreground colours for text.

| CSS variable                      | Colour      | Example                                                 |
| :-------------------------------- | :---------- | :------------------------------------------------------ |
| `var(--ams-color-text)`           | neutral-100 | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-text)" />           |
| `var(--ams-color-text-secondary)` | neutral-60  | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-text-secondary)" /> |
| `var(--ams-color-text-inverse)`   | neutral-0   | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-text-inverse)" />   |

### Background

We have two background colours.
One for the HTML `<body>` and Page, the other for all other components.
They are the same colour in Spacious Mode, but not in Compact Mode.

#### Spacious Mode

| CSS variable                       | Colour    | Example                         |
| :--------------------------------- | :-------- | :------------------------------ |
| `var(--ams-color-background)`      | neutral-0 | <ColorSample value="#ffffff" /> |
| `var(--ams-color-background-body)` | neutral-0 | <ColorSample value="#ffffff" /> |

#### Compact Mode

The background colour of pages is light grey in Compact Mode.
The cells of the Grid have a white background again.
This is because the limited white space makes it more difficult to distinguish cells from each other.

| CSS variable                       | Colour     | Example                         |
| :--------------------------------- | :--------- | :------------------------------ |
| `var(--ams-color-background)`      | neutral-0  | <ColorSample value="#ffffff" /> |
| `var(--ams-color-background-body)` | neutral-10 | <ColorSample value="#e8e8e8" /> |

### Feedback

Use these colours for positive and negative feedback, warnings, and messages that need attention.
Interactive elements in an invalid state use the ‘invalid interactive’ token, not the ‘error feedback’ token.

| CSS variable                        | Colour    | Example                                                   |
| :---------------------------------- | :-------- | :-------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-color-feedback-error)`   | red-60    | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-feedback-error)" />   |
| `var(--ams-color-feedback-info)`    | azure-60  | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-feedback-info)" />    |
| `var(--ams-color-feedback-success)` | green-60  | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-feedback-success)" /> |
| `var(--ams-color-feedback-warning)` | orange-60 | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-feedback-warning)" /> |

### Interactive

These colours show elements that the user can interact with, like links, buttons, and form controls.

| CSS variable                                 | Colour      | Example                                                            |
| :------------------------------------------- | :---------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-color-interactive)`               | blue-60     | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-interactive)" />               |
| `var(--ams-color-interactive-hover)`         | blue-80     | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-interactive-hover)" />         |
| `var(--ams-color-interactive-contrast)`      | neutral-100 | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-interactive-contrast)" />      |
| `var(--ams-color-interactive-disabled)`      | neutral-60  | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-interactive-disabled)" />      |
| `var(--ams-color-interactive-invalid)`       | red-60      | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-interactive-invalid)" />       |
| `var(--ams-color-interactive-invalid-hover)` | red-80      | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-interactive-invalid-hover)" /> |
| `var(--ams-color-interactive-inverse)`       | neutral-0   | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-interactive-inverse)" />       |

### Highlight

Components can use these colours to stand out.

| CSS variable                         | Colour     | Example                                                    |
| :----------------------------------- | :--------- | :--------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-color-highlight-azure)`   | azure-60   | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-highlight-azure)" />   |
| `var(--ams-color-highlight-green)`   | green-60   | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-highlight-green)" />   |
| `var(--ams-color-highlight-lime)`    | lime-60    | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-highlight-lime)" />    |
| `var(--ams-color-highlight-magenta)` | magenta-60 | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-highlight-magenta)" /> |
| `var(--ams-color-highlight-orange)`  | orange-60  | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-highlight-orange)" />  |
| `var(--ams-color-highlight-purple)`  | purple-60  | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-highlight-purple)" />  |
| `var(--ams-color-highlight-yellow)`  | yellow-60  | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-highlight-yellow)" />  |

### Progress

These colours can be used to indicate the progress of a task.

| CSS variable                          | Colour     | Example                                                     |
| :------------------------------------ | :--------- | :---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-color-progress-current)`   | green-60   | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-progress-current)" />   |
| `var(--ams-color-progress-completed)` | green-60   | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-progress-completed)" /> |
| `var(--ams-color-progress-upcoming)`  | neutral-60 | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-progress-upcoming)" />  |

### Separator

This colour is used to separate components or space.

| CSS variable                 | Colour     | Example                                            |
| :--------------------------- | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-color-separator)` | neutral-20 | <ColorSample value="var(--ams-color-separator)" /> |

## Pairing foreground with background colours

### White background

Most of the background of our pages is white.
Both body text and headers are black here, and all kinds of links are blue.

- Text: `var(--ams-color-text)`
- Links: `var(--ams-color-interactive)`

Obviously, these colours are not always effective on other backgrounds.

### Light background

We consider <InlineColorSample color="lime" /> **lime**, <InlineColorSample color="orange" /> **orange** and <InlineColorSample color="yellow" /> **yellow** to be ‘light’ backgrounds.
Black text still provides enough contrast here.
Links on these backgrounds are black as well – this is the **‘contrast’** interactive colour.
The underline and cursor still distinguish links from static text.

- Text: `var(--ams-color-text)`
- Links: `var(--ams-color-interactive-contrast)`

### Dark background

An <InlineColorSample color="azure" /> **azure**, <InlineColorSample color="blue" /> **blue**, <InlineColorSample color="green" /> **green**, <InlineColorSample color="magenta" /> **magenta**, <InlineColorSample color="purple" /> **purple** or <InlineColorSample color="red" /> **red** background is ‘dark’.
On these, both text and links are white – the **‘inverse’** text and interactive colour.

- Text: `var(--ams-color-text-inverse)`
- Links: `var(--ams-color-interactive-inverse)`

Brand/Design Tokens/Cursor

brand-design-tokens-cursor--docs · ./src/brand/design-tokens/cursor.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

<Meta title="Brand/Design Tokens/Cursor" />

# Cursor

[Interactive elements](/docs/docs-developer-guide-interactivity--docs) that do not have a cursor style, such as buttons, should use one of these `cursor` values.

| CSS variable                    | Value         | When to use                                                               |
| :------------------------------ | :------------ | :------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `var(--ams-cursor-interactive)` | `pointer`     | All interactive elements in their default state that lack a cursor style. |
| `var(--ams-cursor-disabled)`    | `not-allowed` | Interactive elements that are disabled.                                   |

Brand/Design Tokens/Focus

brand-design-tokens-focus--docs · ./src/brand/design-tokens/focus.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

<Meta title="Brand/Design Tokens/Focus" />

# Focus

## Token

Click on the table, then hit the Tab key two times to show the outline on the link and button.
On a touch device, use a screen reader or keyboard to place focus.

| CSS variable                      |  Value   | Example link                                            | Example button                                                     |
| :-------------------------------- | :------: | :------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `var(--ams-focus-outline-offset)` | 0.125rem | <a href="#" className="ams-link">A random link text</a> | <button className="ams-button ams-button--primary">Button</button> |

## Design

We use the standard focus indicator of the web browsers.
We only adjust the focus outline with an offset of 2 pixels to increase contrast.

## Guidelines

When setting up a page, ensure that the interactive elements are in a logical order, usually from top to bottom and from left to right.

Brand/Design Tokens/Inputs

brand-design-tokens-inputs--docs · ./src/brand/design-tokens/inputs.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

<Meta title="Brand/Design Tokens/Inputs" />

# Inputs

Several input components share the same appearance in various aspects.
This set of common tokens maintains their visual consistency.

These tokens are used by
[Date Input](/docs/components-forms-date-input--docs),
[File Input](/docs/components-forms-file-input--docs),
[Password Input](/docs/components-forms-password-input--docs),
[Search Field](/docs/components-forms-search-field--docs),
[Select](/docs/components-forms-select--docs),
[Text Area](/docs/components-forms-text-area--docs),
[Text Input](/docs/components-forms-text-input--docs),
[Time Input](/docs/components-forms-time-input--docs) and
(one of them) by [Button](/docs/components-buttons-button--docs).

## Default

| CSS variable                         | Value                                         | Description                                                                                                         |
| :----------------------------------- | :-------------------------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `var(--ams-inputs-background-color)` | `var(--ams-color-background)`                 | The background colour is set explicitly to our general background colour – white.                                   |
| `var(--ams-inputs-border-color)`     | `currentColor`                                | The border always follows the text colour. Using `currentColor` makes this easy.                                    |
| `var(--ams-inputs-border-style)`     | `solid`                                       | Borders are continuous.                                                                                             |
| `var(--ams-inputs-border-width)`     | `var(--ams-border-width-m)`                   | The borders have a medium thickness.                                                                                |
| `var(--ams-inputs-color)`            | `var(--ams-color-text)`                       | Inputs use the default text colour.                                                                                 |
| `var(--ams-inputs-font-family)`      | `var(--ams-typography-font-family)`           | Inputs use our single font family.                                                                                  |
| `var(--ams-inputs-font-size)`        | `var(--ams-typography-body-text-font-size)`   | Inputs use the same font size as body text.                                                                         |
| `var(--ams-inputs-font-weight)`      | `var(--ams-typography-body-text-font-weight)` | Inputs use the same font weight as body text.                                                                       |
| `var(--ams-inputs-line-height)`      | `1.4`                                         | Inputs have their own line height, smaller than that of body text. Buttons also use this to make them equally tall. |
| `var(--ams-inputs-outline-offset)`   | `var(--ams-focus-outline-offset)`             | The offset for the focus outline reuses the general token for it.                                                   |
| `var(--ams-inputs-padding-block)`    | `var(--ams-space-s)`                          | Inputs have small vertical whitespace between their borders and content.                                            |
| `var(--ams-inputs-padding-inline)`   | `var(--ams-space-m)`                          | Inputs have medium horizontal whitespace between their borders and content.                                         |

## Disabled

| CSS variable                       | Value                                   | Description                                                                 |
| :--------------------------------- | :-------------------------------------- | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-inputs-disabled-color)` | `var(--ams-color-interactive-disabled)` | The text colour of a disabled input uses our general colour for this state. |

## Hover

| CSS variable                         | Value                                   | Description                                                                            |
| :----------------------------------- | :-------------------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-inputs-hover-box-shadow)` | `inset 0 0 0 var(--ams-border-width-s)` | When hovering, the border slightly grows to the inside, also following `currentColor`. |

## Invalid

| CSS variable                                   | Value                                                                                | Description                                                                                                          |
| :--------------------------------------------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-inputs-invalid-border-color)`       | `var(--ams-color-interactive-invalid)`                                               | The border of a Text Input with an invalid value uses the invalid interactive colour.                                |
| `var(--ams-inputs-invalid-hover-border-color)` | `var(--ams-color-interactive-hover)`                                                 | When hovering, it uses the hover variant of the invalid interactive colour.                                          |
| `var(--ams-inputs-invalid-hover-box-shadow)`   | `inset 0 0 0 var(--ams-border-width-s)` `var(--ams-color-interactive-invalid-hover)` | When hovering, the border slightly grows to the inside, using the hover variation of the invalid interactive colour. |

## Placeholder

| CSS variable                          | Value                             | Description                                        |
| :------------------------------------ | :-------------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-inputs-placeholder-color)` | `var(--ams-color-text-secondary)` | A placeholder text uses the secondary text colour. |

## How to use

Use these tokens if you recreate our React components with different technology, or if you create a new input component.

Brand/Design Tokens/Links

brand-design-tokens-links--docs · ./src/brand/design-tokens/links.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

<Meta title="Brand/Design Tokens/Links" />

# Links

Several link components share the same appearance in various aspects.
This set of common tokens maintains their visual consistency.

## Default

Our default links have the following styles:

| CSS variable                                       | Value                                | Description                                                                  |
| :------------------------------------------------- | :----------------------------------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-links-color)`                           | `var(--ams-color-interactive)`       | The default colour for links is our interactive colour.                      |
| `var(--ams-links-text-decoration-thickness)`       | `0.125rem`                           | Link underlines are 2 pixels thick.                                          |
| `var(--ams-links-text-underline-offset)`           | `0.15625rem`                         | The underline is positioned 2.5 pixels below the text.                       |
| `var(--ams-links-hover-color)`                     | `var(--ams-color-interactive-hover)` | The hover colour is our interactive hover colour.                            |
| `var(--ams-links-hover-text-decoration-thickness)` | `0.1875rem`                          | The underline thickness grows to 3px on hover.                               |
| `var(--ams-links-hover-text-underline-offset)`     | `0.09375rem`                         | The offset shrinks accordingly, making the underline look like it is rising. |

## Subtle

Subtle links are used in places where the context helps indicate that the text is a link.
For instance, in the Page Header or Pagination components.
As a result, their styling can be less obvious.

| CSS variable                                         | Value       | Description                                                                                                          |
| :--------------------------------------------------- | :---------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-links-subtle-text-decoration-line)`       | `none`      | Subtle links do not show an underline by default. The surrounding context should clearly indicate that it is a link. |
| `var(--ams-links-subtle-hover-text-decoration-line)` | `underline` | An underline is shown on hover.                                                                                      |

## Contrast

Links on light (but not white) backgrounds use the interactive contrast colour.

| CSS variable                            | Value                                   | Description                                                                                                   |
| :-------------------------------------- | :-------------------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `var(--ams-links-contrast-color)`       | `var(--ams-color-interactive-contrast)` | On light backgrounds, the interactive contrast colour is used.                                                |
| `var(--ams-links-contrast-hover-color)` | `var(--ams-color-interactive-contrast)` | The same colour is used on hover. We currently do not show a difference between the default and hover states. |

## Inverse

On dark backgrounds, links have the inverse colour.

| CSS variable                           | Value                                  | Description                                                                                                   |
| :------------------------------------- | :------------------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `var(--ams-links-inverse-color)`       | `var(--ams-color-interactive-inverse)` | On dark backgrounds, the interactive inverse colour is used.                                                  |
| `var(--ams-links-inverse-hover-color)` | `var(--ams-color-interactive-inverse)` | The same colour is used on hover. We currently do not show a difference between the default and hover states. |

## How to use

Use these tokens if you recreate our React components with different technology, or if you create a new link component.

Brand/Design Tokens/Space

brand-design-tokens-space--docs · ./src/brand/design-tokens/space.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import { SpaceSample } from "../../_components/SpaceSample/SpaceSample";

<Meta title="Brand/Design Tokens/Space" />

# Space

We have a single scale of spacing values for all kinds of whitespace.

## Spacious

Since our typography is fluid, spacing is as well.
Medium space matches the font size of regular body text.
There are two smaller and three larger options available.
The two biggest sizes are mainly used for the grid; they grow faster in wider windows.

| CSS variable           | ≤&nbsp;320 | Example                         | ≥&nbsp;1440 | Example                          |
| :--------------------- | :--------: | :------------------------------ | :---------: | :------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-space-xs)`  |     4      | <SpaceSample value="0.25rem" /> |      6      | <SpaceSample value="0.375rem" /> |
| `var(--ams-space-s)`   |     8      | <SpaceSample value="0.5rem" />  |     12      | <SpaceSample value="0.75rem" />  |
| `var(--ams-space-m)`   |     16     | <SpaceSample value="1rem" />    |     24      | <SpaceSample value="1.5rem" />   |
| `var(--ams-space-l)`   |     24     | <SpaceSample value="1.5rem" />  |     36      | <SpaceSample value="2.25rem" />  |
| `var(--ams-space-xl)`  |     36     | <SpaceSample value="2.25rem" /> |     60      | <SpaceSample value="3.75rem" />  |
| `var(--ams-space-2xl)` |     48     | <SpaceSample value="3rem" />    |     90      | <SpaceSample value="5.625rem" /> |

## Compact

While typography in [Compact Mode](/docs/docs-developer-guide-modes--docs) isn't responsive, spacing is.
This helps make better use of small screens.

| CSS variable           | ≤&nbsp;320 | Example                         | ≥&nbsp;1440 | Example                         |
| :--------------------- | :--------: | :------------------------------ | :---------: | :------------------------------ |
| `var(--ams-space-xs)`  |     4      | <SpaceSample value="0.25rem" /> |      4      | <SpaceSample value="0.25rem" /> |
| `var(--ams-space-s)`   |     8      | <SpaceSample value="0.5rem" />  |      8      | <SpaceSample value="0.5rem" />  |
| `var(--ams-space-m)`   |     12     | <SpaceSample value="0.75rem" /> |     16      | <SpaceSample value="1rem" />    |
| `var(--ams-space-l)`   |     16     | <SpaceSample value="1rem" />    |     24      | <SpaceSample value="1.5rem" />  |
| `var(--ams-space-xl)`  |     24     | <SpaceSample value="1.5rem" />  |     36      | <SpaceSample value="2.25rem" /> |
| `var(--ams-space-2xl)` |     32     | <SpaceSample value="2rem" />    |     48      | <SpaceSample value="3rem" />    |

## Pixels and more

We use pixels in our documentation on sizes to convey a more accurate mental image.
In code, sizes are defined in relative units (like `rem`, `em`, `ex`, `vw`, `vh`, and `ch`) to make components more flexible and accessible.
MDN offers a good overview on these [CSS values and units](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_Values_and_Units).
The responsive values above are specified using the [clamp function](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/clamp).

Brand/Design Tokens/Typography

brand-design-tokens-typography--docs · ./src/brand/design-tokens/typography.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import { TypographySample } from "../../_components/TypographySample/TypographySample";

<Meta title="Brand/Design Tokens/Typography" />

# Typography

We have a fluid typography system.
The options for the font family, font size, line height and font weight come in two groups: body text and headings.

## Body text

### Font size

The size of text adjusts to the width of the window for pleasant reading on screens of any size.

| CSS variable                                        | ≤&nbsp;320 | Example                                                                                                  | ≥&nbsp;1440 | Example                                                                                                  |
| :-------------------------------------------------- | :--------: | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :---------: | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-small-font-size)`   |     16     | <TypographySample fontSize="1rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-small-line-height)" />      |     16      | <TypographySample fontSize="1rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-small-line-height)" />      |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-font-size)`         |     18     | <TypographySample fontSize="1.125rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-line-height)" />        |     20      | <TypographySample fontSize="1.25rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-line-height)" />         |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-large-font-size)`   |     21     | <TypographySample fontSize="1.3125rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-large-line-height)" /> |     25      | <TypographySample fontSize="1.5625rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-large-line-height)" /> |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-x-large-font-size)` |     24     | <TypographySample fontSize="1.5rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-x-large-line-height)" />  |     32      | <TypographySample fontSize="2rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-x-large-line-height)" />    |

### Line height

The line heights have been designed to match the corresponding font size.
Do not combine tokens of different sizes.

| CSS variable                                          | Value |
| :---------------------------------------------------- | :---: |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-small-line-height)`   |  1.5  |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-line-height)`         |  1.6  |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-large-line-height)`   |  1.5  |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-x-large-line-height)` |  1.4  |

### Font weight

The font weight can either be regular or bold.

| CSS variable                                       | Value | Example                                                                                                                                                        |
| :------------------------------------------------- | :---: | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-font-weight)`      |  400  | <TypographySample fontSize="1.25rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-line-height)" />      |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-bold-font-weight)` |  800  | <TypographySample fontSize="1.25rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-bold-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-line-height)" /> |

## Headings

### Font size

We support five sizes for headings, although levels 1 to 4 are most commonly used.

| CSS variable                                | ≤&nbsp;320 | Example                                                                                                                                                   | ≥&nbsp;1440 | Example                                                                                                                                                   |
| :------------------------------------------ | :--------: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :---------: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-1-font-size)` |     32     | <TypographySample fontSize="2rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-1-line-height)" />      |     48      | <TypographySample fontSize="3rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-1-line-height)" />      |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-2-font-size)` |     24     | <TypographySample fontSize="1.5rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-2-line-height)" />    |     32      | <TypographySample fontSize="2rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-2-line-height)" />      |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-3-font-size)` |     21     | <TypographySample fontSize="1.3125rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-3-line-height)" /> |     25      | <TypographySample fontSize="1.5625rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-3-line-height)" /> |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-4-font-size)` |     18     | <TypographySample fontSize="1.125rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-4-line-height)" />  |     20      | <TypographySample fontSize="1.25rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-4-line-height)" />   |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-5-font-size)` |     16     | <TypographySample fontSize="1rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-5-line-height)" />      |     16      | <TypographySample fontSize="1rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-5-line-height)" />      |

### Line height

Headings use smaller line heights than body text.

| CSS variable                                  | Value |
| :-------------------------------------------- | :---: |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-1-line-height)` |  1.2  |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-2-line-height)` |  1.3  |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-3-line-height)` |  1.3  |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-4-line-height)` |  1.4  |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-5-line-height)` |  1.4  |

### Font weight

Headings only use the bold font weight.

| CSS variable                                | Value | Example                                                                                                                                                   |
| :------------------------------------------ | :---: | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)` |  800  | <TypographySample fontSize="1.5625rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-3-line-height)" /> |

## Other aspects

### Font family

All text appears in our Amsterdam Sans font.
If it is not available, we use Arial or the generic sans-serif instead.

| CSS variable                        |                Value                |
| :---------------------------------- | :---------------------------------: |
| `var(--ams-typography-font-family)` | 'Amsterdam Sans', Arial, sans-serif |

### Hyphenation

Words that do not fit on a single line will be split with a hyphen and continue on the next line.
We let the browser decide when and how to hyphenate words.
This is why it is important to [set the correct language](/docs/docs-developer-guide-language--docs#specify-the-language-for-the-entire-document) for the website.

| CSS variable                                  | Value |
| :-------------------------------------------- | :---- |
| `var(--ams-typography-hyphenate-limit-chars)` | auto  |

### Underlining

We only underline hyperlinks.
To see how, consult our [tokens for links](/docs/brand-design-tokens-links--docs).

### Letter spacing

Kerning has been optimized in the font file.
We do not adjust the space between glyphs.

## Compact Mode

Our large font sizes for public websites would be counterproductive in internal websites.
They use [Compact Mode](/docs/docs-developer-guide-modes--docs), where both body text and headings are considerably smaller.

### Body text

#### Font size

| CSS variable                                        | ≤&nbsp;320 | Example                                                                                                          | ≥&nbsp;1440 | Example                                                                                                            |
| :-------------------------------------------------- | :--------: | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :---------: | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-small-font-size)`   |     14     | <TypographySample compact fontSize="0.875rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-small-line-height)" />  |     14      | <TypographySample compact fontSize="0.875rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-small-line-height)" />    |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-font-size)`         |     16     | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-line-height)" />            |     16      | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-line-height)" />              |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-large-font-size)`   |     18     | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1.125rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-large-line-height)" />  |     19      | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1.1875rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-large-line-height)" />   |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-x-large-font-size)` |     20     | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1.25rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-x-large-line-height)" /> |     23      | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1.4375rem" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-body-text-x-large-line-height)" /> |

### Line height

| CSS variable                                          | Value |
| :---------------------------------------------------- | :---: |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-small-line-height)`   |  1.4  |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-line-height)`         |  1.5  |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-large-line-height)`   |  1.4  |
| `var(--ams-typography-body-text-x-large-line-height)` |  1.3  |

### Headings

#### Font size

| CSS variable                                | ≤&nbsp;320 | Example                                                                                                                                                          | ≥&nbsp;1440 | Example                                                                                                                                                           |
| :------------------------------------------ | :--------: | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :---------: | :---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-1-font-size)` |     24     | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1.5rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-1-line-height)" />   |     28      | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1.75rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-1-line-height)" />   |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-2-font-size)` |     20     | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1.25rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-2-line-height)" />  |     23      | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1.4375rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-2-line-height)" /> |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-3-font-size)` |     18     | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1.125rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-3-line-height)" /> |     19      | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1.1875rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-3-line-height)" /> |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-4-font-size)` |     16     | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-4-line-height)" />     |     16      | <TypographySample compact fontSize="1rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-4-line-height)" />      |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-5-font-size)` |     14     | <TypographySample compact fontSize="0.875rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-5-line-height)" /> |     14      | <TypographySample compact fontSize="0.875rem" fontWeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-font-weight)" lineHeight="var(--ams-typography-heading-5-line-height)" />  |

### Line height

| CSS variable                                  | Value |
| :-------------------------------------------- | :---: |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-1-line-height)` |  1.2  |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-2-line-height)` |  1.3  |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-3-line-height)` |  1.3  |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-4-line-height)` |  1.4  |
| `var(--ams-typography-heading-5-line-height)` |  1.4  |

Pages/Introduction

pages-introduction--docs · ./src/pages/Introduction.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

<Meta title="Pages/Introduction" />

# Pages

This section demonstrates how to compose pages with the Design System’s components.
It uses the latest versions of the libraries, without any customizations.
This gives an honest representation of what they can achieve and how they work in the browser.

## Documentation

We offer examples for various page types; see the list in the sidebar.
We separate templates for public websites from those for internal ones.

The ‘Docs’ page for each page explains its design decisions and how to build it.

## Examples

The ‘Default’ example shows the actual page in its entirety.
Some page types have more than one example.

### Flexible content

Reload an example to get new random texts and images.
Use the ‘Control’ tab to see how it looks with your own content.

### Test for accessibility and see HTML output

The ‘Accessibility’ tab offers a report of various automatic tests.
The ‘HTML’ tab displays the entire document as rendered by all components on the page.

### Check how the page would look on various devices

The buttons in the toolbar offer various features, of which the ‘three-layered square’ button replicates the appearance and behaviour of the page on a phone, tablet or desktop.
Press the button with the two arrows to rotate the screen.

### See the page full-screen

Use the ‘Go to full screen’ (or press `alt+F`) or ‘Open canvas in new tab’ buttons to the top right to see the entire page.

Pages/Public/Introduction

pages-public-introduction--docs · ./src/pages/public/Introduction.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

<Meta title="Pages/Public/Introduction" />

# Public websites

The City maintains numerous websites for various target audiences: citizens, entrepreneurs and visitors.
This section presents a number of page types for websites of which Amsterdam is the primary sender, and the intended audience is the general public or a specific part of it.

## Spacious Mode

Public websites must use the default Spacious Mode, which applies generous
[typography](/docs/brand-design-tokens-typography--docs) and [spacing](/docs/brand-design-tokens-space--docs).
This appearance keeps the focus on the content.
It makes information easy to read and helps users find what they need without distractions.

## Page structure

The general structure for a page is:

```tsx
<Page>
  <SkipLink href="#inhoud">Direct naar inhoud</SkipLink>
  <PageHeader>
    <Grid />
  </PageHeader>
  <Grid>
    <Breadcrumb />
  </Grid>
  <Grid as="main" id="inhoud">
    …
  </Grid>
  <PageFooter>
    <PageFooter.Spotlight>
      <Grid />
    </PageFooter.Spotlight>
    <PageFooter.Menu />
  </PageFooter>
</Page>
```

### No Menu

Public websites cannot use the [Menu](/docs/components-navigation-menu--docs) component.
They should offer navigation using the Page Header only.

## How to size the Grid Cells

Everything on the page is wrapped in a [Grid Cell](/docs/components-layout-grid--docs).
Its `span` and `start` props let you define where all sections go on the page.
Do so for all variants of the Grid (narrow, medium, and wide: 4, 8, and 12 columns).

Some typical page sections always use the same values:

### Content page header

The header section contains the page title, publication date, optional metadata, and the lead paragraph.
It is a bit **wider** than the body section and is positioned slightly further **to the left**.

```tsx
<Grid.Cell span={{ narrow: 4, medium: 7, wide: 9 }} start={{ narrow: 1, medium: 1, wide: 2 }} />
```

Breadcrumbs use their own Grid placed directly above the header, using the same cell configuration.

### Content page body

The body section contains the main textual content of the page.
It spans **7 columns** on the wide grid and **6 columns** on the medium-wide grid to maintain an optimal line length for readability.
This also applies to forms.

```tsx
<Grid.Cell span={{ narrow: 4, medium: 6, wide: 7 }} start={{ narrow: 1, medium: 2, wide: 3 }} />
```

### Side-by-side blocks

Link Blocks (a section with a header, paragraph, and standalone link) can be displayed **side by side**, for example within a Spotlight.
To achieve this, use the following configuration.
The `start` prop is not needed here.

```tsx
<Grid.Cell span={{ narrow: 4, medium: 4, wide: 6 }} />
```

On navigation pages, similar sections use Link Lists and are 1 column **narrower** on wide screens.
When rendering them in a loop, the `start` position is only necessary for the odd cells:

```tsx
<Grid.Cell span={{ narrow: 4, medium: 4, wide: 5 }} start={index % 2 ? undefined : { narrow: 1, medium: 1, wide: 2 }} />
```

### Top tasks

Top-task overviews use Cards that contain only a heading and a paragraph, without an image.
On the wide grid, each Card spans 3 columns, fitting **4 per row**.
On the narrow and medium-wide grids, each Card spans 4 columns.
The `start` prop is not needed here.

```tsx
<Grid.Cell span={{ narrow: 4, medium: 4, wide: 3 }} />
```

### Preview cards

Index pages such as news overviews or search results use Cards as well for navigation.
They usually do include the image of the target content.
These layouts require **4 columns** for each Card, which can be achieved with a single spanning value:

```tsx
<Grid.Cell span={4} />
```

## Related components

- [Breadcrumb](/docs/components-navigation-breadcrumb--docs)
- [Grid](/docs/components-layout-grid--docs)
- [Page Footer](/docs/components-containers-page-footer--docs)
- [Page Header](/docs/components-containers-page-header--docs)
- [Page](/docs/components-containers-page--docs)
- [Skip Link](/docs/components-navigation-skip-link--docs)

Pages/Internal/Introduction

pages-internal-introduction--docs · ./src/pages/internal/Introduction.docs.mdx
{/* @license CC0-1.0 */}

import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

<Meta title="Pages/Internal/Introduction" />

# Internal websites

The design system also supports internal websites, such as backoffice systems used by our staff.

These typically require higher information density and faster task execution.
The guidelines below describe how to apply the design in these contexts.

## Compact Mode

Internal websites frequently use [Compact Mode](/docs/docs-developer-guide-modes--docs), which applies reduced sizes for [typography](/docs/brand-design-tokens-typography--docs) and [spacing](/docs/brand-design-tokens-space--docs).
This mode increases information density and improves the use of available screen space.

Compact Mode automatically adjusts all components.
As a result, they remain consistent and equally suitable for task-oriented workflows as they are for content-driven public websites.

## Page structure

The general structure for a page is:

```tsx
<Page withMenu>
  <SkipLink className="ams-page__area--skip-link" href="#inhoud">
    Direct naar inhoud
  </SkipLink>
  <PageHeader className="ams-page__area--header" noMenuButtonOnWideWindow>
    <Menu />
  </PageHeader>
  <Menu className="ams-page__area--menu" inWideWindow />
  <main className="ams-page__area--body" id="inhoud">
    <Grid />
  </main>
  <PageFooter className="ams-page__area--footer" />
</Page>
```

### Positioning classes

All children of `Page` have a class name starting with `ams-page__area--` to lay them out correctly on the underlying grid.

### With menu

Internal websites may use a [Menu](/docs/components-navigation-menu--docs) to support navigation across major functional areas or modules.
This pattern helps users move efficiently between different parts of the website while keeping the structure clear and predictable.

The component must be rendered twice.

1. One instance, for narrow and medium windows, sits in the Page Header.
   The user can access it through the menu button.
   Hide the menu button on wide windows.
2. A second Menu goes between the Page Header and the main content.
   This is for wide windows, where the menu is positioned to the side.
   The `inWideWindow` prop ensures that only one of both components is present at all times.

Make sure that both menus have the same items.

## Related components

- [Breadcrumb](/docs/components-navigation-breadcrumb--docs)
- [Grid](/docs/components-layout-grid--docs)
- [Menu](/docs/components-navigation-menu--docs)
- [Page Footer](/docs/components-containers-page-footer--docs)
- [Page Header](/docs/components-containers-page-header--docs)
- [Page](/docs/components-containers-page--docs)
- [Skip Link](/docs/components-navigation-skip-link--docs)
